request.question: Is the SAR 55M of Q4 one-offs genuinely non-recurring? Break it down.

events.text: Lead answer: No, treating the full
SAR 55.4m as clean, non-recurring is too generous. On my read of
the components, only roughly SAR 20m is genuinely one-off; the balance (~SAR 35
m) sits in line items that are recurring by nature and, in the case of the
ECL, has already partially repeated in Q1 2026. Judgment flag appl

ied throughout, I'll mark what would change my view.

**The four components (management's dis
closure)**
Red Color recorded SAR 12.2m losses related to impairment in the group's portfolio companies; go
odwill impairment of SAR 11.8m related to Marn; Others Segment ECL of SAR 23.4m; and Snoonu deal expenses of ~SAR 8m.

events.quote:
Key highlights for Q4 - 2025
Non-KSA 
Platforms
• Snoonuto serve as Jahez Group’s international arm for all non-KSA operations
• 18% gross profit margin supported by improvement in ads revenue, co

events.text: The industry-pack cross-check corroborates the same split,
SAR 23.4m credit-loss provisions, SAR 11.8m goodwill impairment, SAR 12m investment losses, SAR 8m acquis
ition costs.

events.quote:
Jahez CEO: Q4 impacted by SAR 55M one-off items, revenue may rise 7–21% in 2026
Argaam · 31 Mar 2026 · argaam.com/en/article/articledetail/id/1892181
Q4 2025 profit was dented by roughly SAR 55m of

events.text:
**Item-by-item recurrence assessment**

**1. Snoonu deal expenses ~SAR 8m, genuin
ely non-recurring.** M&A transaction costs tied to a one-time acquisition close. Clean add-back. No basis to exp

ect repetition.

**2. Marn goodwill impairment SAR 11.8m, non-recurring as a charge, but a red flag on
underlying value.** You cannot impair the same goodwill twice, so the SAR 11.8m charge itself won't recur. But it
is a non-cash write-down signalling deterioration in the Marn cash-generating unit's outlook. That the
business needs fixing is confirmed one quarter later:
Net Revenue of SAR
22.8m in Q1 2026, down 7.2% YoY mainly due to Marn restructuring & strategy revision; Marn repositioning toward higher-quality merch
ant cohorts aimed at improving the underlying credit-risk profile.

events.quote:
Key highlights for Q 1 - 2026
Non - KSA 
Platforms
• Qatar ( Snoonu ) continues to be the main growth engine, delivering strong GMV and strong multi verticality results 
• Bahrain & Kuwait – focus

events.text: Judgment: I'd add the charge back

but not treat the impairment as evidence the Marn problem is behind them.

**3. Red
Color / portfolio fair-value losses SAR 12.2m, recurring in nature, not a true one-off.** These are f
air-value marks on a corporate-VC (FVTPL) portfolio, which swing every period. For the full year,
fair value differences were (11,308,115)

events.quote:
Movement in investments is as follows: 
31 March
2026
31 December 
2025
31 March 
2025
Balance at the beginning of the period/ year 85,813,977 68,296,898 68,296,898
Additions during the period

events.text: in FY2025, i.e
. the "one-off" is essentially the whole-year mark landing in Q4. These marks can move
either direction: Q1 2026 booked zero FVTPL movement versus a SAR 5.
2m loss in Q1 2025. Labelling a volatile mark-to-market line as non-recurring is not defensible; it is
a normal, recurring source of P&L noise for a company running a venture book

.

**4. Others-segment ECL SAR 23.4m, the weakest "one-off" claim, and it has already partly repeated.** This is the largest chunk.
At the segment level,
the YoY decline was primarily driven by a significant increase in expected credit losses (ECL), which r
ose to SAR 29.4 million in FY 2025 from SAR 0.5 million in the prior year.

events.quote:
The YoY decline was primarily 
driven by a significant increase in expected credit losses (ECL), which rose to  2 9 .4 million in FY 2025 from 
0.5 million in the prior year.

events.text: Two re
asons I do not treat this as clean: (a) at Group level, ECL is a standing add-back line,
not an exceptional item,
ECL Adjustment SAR 31.5m in FY2025 versus SAR 13.8
m in FY2024

events.quote:
8 ) pp
Other Income/Expense (9.4) (39.9) (76.3 %)
Depreciation & Amortization 89.4 51.0 + 75.4%
Financing Cost & Zakat 12.5 24.9 (49.9%)
ECL Adjustment 31.5 13.8 + 128.3 %
Other Mgmt.

events.text: ; and (b) it recurred in Q1 2026, where the Others segment again car
ried an ECL charge of SAR 5.0m within a Group impairment loss on trade receivables of SA
R 7.7m. The Marn credit-quality repositioning noted above tells you the receivables problem is structural to the merchant base, not a

single bad-debt event.

**Analyst bottom line**

- Genuinely non-recurring: Snoonu

deal costs (~SAR 8m) and the goodwill-impairment charge itself (SAR 11.8m) → ~SAR 20m.
- Recurring/
volatile by nature: FVTPL marks (SAR 12.2m) and ECL (SAR 23.4m) → ~SAR 35m,

of which the ECL has demonstrably continued into Q1 2026.

For normalisation purposes I would
strip out roughly SAR 20m, not SAR 55m, and haircut Q
4 "adjusted" profitability accordingly. What would change this view: (i) evidence the
Marn merchant-book ECL genuinely normalises toward the FY2024 ~SAR 0.5m run-rate rather than the
elevated Q1 2026 level, and (ii) the Red Color portfolio marks stabilising rather than swinging. Note
management does not present these as "exceptional/non-recurring" in the audited reconciliation,
the reconciliation lists them as standing "ECL Adjustment" and "Other Mgmt. Adjust
ments" add-backs

events.quote:
8 ) pp
Other Income/Expense (9.4) (39.9) (76.3 %)
Depreciation & Amortization 89.4 51.0 + 75.4%
Financing Cost & Zakat 12.5 24.9 (49.9%)
ECL Adjustment 31.5 13.8 + 128.3 %
Other Mgmt. Adjustments

events.text: , which is itself a tell that at least the ECL is treated as an ongoing, if
lumpy, cost of the business.
